Package com.shanebeestudios.vf.api.recipe

Class FurnaceRecipe

java.lang.Object
com.shanebeestudios.vf.api.recipe.Recipe
com.shanebeestudios.vf.api.recipe.FurnaceRecipe
All Implemented Interfaces:
net.kyori.adventure.key.Keyed, Keyed
public class FurnaceRecipe extends Recipe
Recipes for Furnaces

  • Constructor Details

    • FurnaceRecipe

      public FurnaceRecipe(NamespacedKey key, Material ingredient, Material result, int cookTime)
      Create a new recipe for a Furnace

      The experience for this will default to 0.0

      Parameters:
      key - Key for recipe
      ingredient - Ingredient to be put into furnace
      result - The resulting item from this recipe
      cookTime - Time to cook this item (in ticks)

    • FurnaceRecipe

      public FurnaceRecipe(NamespacedKey key, Material ingredient, Material result, int cookTime, float experience)
      Create a new recipe for a Furnace
      Parameters:
      key - Key for recipe
      ingredient - Ingredient to be put into furnace
      result - The resulting item from this recipe
      cookTime - Time to cook this item (in ticks)
      experience - The experience the player will receive for cooking this item

  • Method Details

    • getVanillaFurnaceRecipes

      public static List<FurnaceRecipe> getVanillaFurnaceRecipes()
      Get a list of vanilla Minecraft furnace recipes
      Returns:
      List of vanilla furnace recipes

    • getVanillaSmokingRecipes

      public static List<FurnaceRecipe> getVanillaSmokingRecipes()
      Get a list of vanilla Minecraft smoking recipes

      NOTE: These recipes are only available on MC 1.14+

      Returns:
      List of vanilla smoking recipes

    • getVanillaBlastingRecipes

      public static List<FurnaceRecipe> getVanillaBlastingRecipes()
      Get a list of vanilla Minecraft blasting recipes

      NOTE: These recipes are only available on MC 1.14+

      Returns:
      List of vanilla blasting recipes

    • getIngredient

      @Deprecated public Material getIngredient()
      Deprecated.
      Use getIngredientType() instead

      Will be working on using ItemStacks for recipes in the future

      Returns:
      Ingredient of this recipe
